classObjective: Write systems of inequalities to represent real-world constraints with two variables, determine whether an ordered pair is a solution without graphing, and write a system of inequalities that corresponds to a given graph.
workedExamples: 7-5, Example 2 — Graph Systems with Horizontal, Vertical, and Standard-Form Boundaries (continued)
7-5, Example 3 — Model Real-World Situations with Systems of Inequalities
warmUp: Write a system for: You need at least 5 hours of sleep and at most 8 hours of screen time
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: exercise program constraints; souvenir budget constraints; feasible region enumeration; substitution checking
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice writing systems of inequalities from real-world contexts and graphs
independentPractice: Practice problems writing systems of inequalities and verifying solutions by substitution
assessment: Exit ticket: write a system of inequalities for a budget problem with spending and savings constraints
capReflection: How do real-world constraints like time or budget limits translate into systems of inequalities?
